Version
  • Home
  • Home

FeederFlow.jl

FeederFlow.jl parses benchmark OpenDSS feeder files, assembles sparse admittance partitions, builds ZIP load operators, and runs a fixed-tap Z-Bus power flow.

Public API

FeederFlow.balanced_slack — Function
balanced_slack()

Return the canonical balanced three-phase slack phasors [1∠0°, 1∠-120°, 1∠120°].

FeederFlow.parse_file — Function
parse_file(path; kwargs...)

Parse an OpenDSS feeder entry file into a NetworkModel.

The parser resolves Redirect/Compile chains, normalizes object names and properties, collects component provenance, and infers per-unit base quantities. Keyword arguments are forwarded to the underlying DSS parser.

FeederFlow.build_y — Function
build_y(network; regulator_model=:nonideal, epsilon=1e-6)

Assemble the feeder nodal admittance partitions (Y, Y_NS, Y_SS) and node order/index maps as a YBusModel.

For IEEE benchmark feeders, assembly follows benchmark-specific conventions for linecode scaling, shunt treatment, and folded regulator branches.

FeederFlow.compute_no_load — Function
compute_no_load(ybus; v_slack=balanced_slack())

Solve the no-load linear system and return network/slack phase voltages.

FeederFlow.build_load_model — Function
build_load_model(network, ybus, noload)

Construct ZIP load operators for the active network nodes and return a LoadModel containing:

  1. nonlinear current-injection contributions (PQ and I models), and
  2. linear admittance stamps (YL) for Z-model loads.
FeederFlow.solve_power_flow — Function
solve_power_flow(bundle; method=:zbus, max_iter=10, tol=1e-5)

Run fixed-point Z-bus iterations using the preassembled analysis bundle and return a PowerFlowResult.

solve_power_flow(network; method=:zbus, regulator_model=:nonideal, epsilon=1e-5, max_iter=10, tol=1e-5)

Build Y-bus and load operators from a parsed NetworkModel, solve power flow, and return an AnalysisBundle.

FeederFlow.solve_case — Function
solve_case(path; kwargs...)

Convenience wrapper for parse_file(path; kwargs...) |> solve_power_flow.

FeederFlow.component_names — Function
component_names(table)

Return component names in the table's deterministic iteration order.

Public Types

FeederFlow.ComponentTable — Type
ComponentTable(items)
ComponentTable{T}(order, data)

Name-indexed, iteration-stable container used for parsed network components. Supports vector-style iteration and string-key lookup.

FeederFlow.DSSParseError — Type
DSSParseError(file, line, object, property, message)

Structured parser exception with source location and OpenDSS object/property context.

FeederFlow.Provenance — Type
Provenance(source_file, object_name, original_properties, command_origin)

Metadata snapshot for a parsed OpenDSS object.

FeederFlow.TerminalSpec — Type
TerminalSpec(bus, phases)

Bus terminal descriptor with bus name and 1-based phase numbers.

FeederFlow.BusPhase — Type
BusPhase(bus, phase)

Single electrical node identifier keyed by bus name and phase number.

FeederFlow.BaseQuantities — Type
BaseQuantities(Sbase, Vbase, Zbase, Ybase)

Per-unit base quantities used across network assembly and solving.

FeederFlow.SourceSpec — Type
SourceSpec(name, bus, phases, basekv, pu)

Specification of the feeder source/slack definition.

FeederFlow.BusSpec — Type
BusSpec(name, phases)

Per-bus phase availability metadata.

FeederFlow.LineCode — Type
LineCode(name, nphases, rmatrix, xmatrix, cmatrix)

Line impedance and capacitance template parsed from OpenDSS linecode objects.

FeederFlow.LineDevice — Type
LineDevice(...)

Concrete line element with resolved geometry/electrical data and provenance.

FeederFlow.TransformerWinding — Type
TransformerWinding(index, bus, conn, kv, kva, resistance, tap)

Single transformer winding specification.

FeederFlow.RegControl — Type
RegControl(...)

Parsed OpenDSS regulator control settings attached to a transformer.

FeederFlow.TransformerDevice — Type
TransformerDevice(...)

Transformer element with winding data and optional regulator control metadata.

FeederFlow.CapacitorDevice — Type
CapacitorDevice(...)

Shunt capacitor element definition.

FeederFlow.LoadDevice — Type
LoadDevice(...)

Load element definition with connection type, OpenDSS model code, and power data.

FeederFlow.NetworkModel — Type
NetworkModel(...)

Parsed feeder model containing component tables, base quantities, and provenance.

FeederFlow.YBusModel — Type
YBusModel(...)

Assembled nodal admittance model with network/slack partitions and node order maps. YL stores constant-impedance load admittance contributions.

FeederFlow.NoLoadResult — Type
NoLoadResult(slack, w, phase_voltages)

No-load voltage solution for network and slack nodes.

FeederFlow.LoadModel — Type
LoadModel(contributions, YL, summary)

Nonlinear load current operators plus constant-impedance load admittance stamps.

FeederFlow.PowerFlowResult — Type
PowerFlowResult(...)

Power-flow solve summary including convergence metadata and solved voltages.

FeederFlow.AnalysisBundle — Type
AnalysisBundle(network, ybus, noload, loads, result)

End-to-end analysis artifact combining parsed input, assembled models, and solution.

Internal utilities

FeederFlow.compute_regulator_secondary_voltages — Function

Compute secondary-side regulator bus voltages after power flow solution.

For IEEE benchmark feeders, these nodes (e.g., 150r, 9r, 25r, 160r) are excluded from the Y-bus but MATLAB computes them post-solve using: Vsecondary = inv(Av) * (Vprimary - Zreg * inv(Ai) * (Yn * Vprimary - Ym * Vdownstream))
